title: Drimia fugax
taxonomy: Drimia fugax is classified within the family Asparagaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as asparaguses or aloe-like plants. This family has undergone various changes in classification over the years, with some sources placing it within the Liliaceae or Hyacinthaceae families. Drimia fugax is further classified within the genus Drimia, which comprises over 60 species of plants native to southern Africa. The classification of Drimia fugax is a complex and evolving field, with ongoing research contributing to our understanding of its relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Drimia fugax, also known as the "false chive," is a plant species in the Asparagaceae family, characterized by its grass-like foliage and small white flowers.
